response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned generalist with strong full-stack and systems experience, but the role demands deep Java/Kotlin/JVM expertise plus genuine interest in IDE internals and the Spring ecosystem. Java appears only briefly (robotic arms, mobile orchestration) and Kotlin/Spring/Hibernate/Quarkus are absent. Prior IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments was in C/C++, not JVM tooling. The skill mismatch on the core required stack is too significant for a mid-senior IC role.
strengths: Long track record on complex, large-scale, performance-sensitive systems (Sociomantic micro-services, low-latency apps)
Strong software craftsmanship discipline — TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development, workshops on testing
Some IDE-adjacent exposure via NI LabWindows/CVI (C IDE tooling for engineers)
concerns: No demonstrated depth in Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, or Quarkus — the core stack of the role
Java experience is shallow and dated (robotic arms, mobile orchestration ~2016-2018), not enterprise/JVM-deep
No evidence of interest or experience in IDE internals or language tooling on the JVM

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and complex-systems track record
